ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree feliŋkum keŋ nyiaa, “Wu n nɔɔ, na n ka lee ɔ man.”
translated: When Jesus heard this, he said, “You also go and do likewise.”

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Mena ne gyae la ke kɔŋ wee ɔtomante keŋ man. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ baa kɔŋ kaa lese balaŋ kumɛɛ baŋ lee balaŋ kpaakpaa baŋ man.
translated: My mother has gone to the farm and my father has also gone to the farm, the children are left at home with grandmother and grandfather.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now am here. I have not come on my own; but he sent me."

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Akpaa nyi Bɛlisɛbu doŋ te mɛɛ mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ na, amɔte doŋ yaa ɛ balaŋ baŋ mɔ na mo gegi feliŋkumɛɛ? Mena na ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ ne gyae la baa bu ŋon fɔɔ.
translated: When the white man came to our land, did he meet any of our gods in our land? We don't know, but it seems that the white man's god is more powerful than ours.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ?” Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae nyi n ke fa me bia bala kɛwɔ gbɛɛ, na ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” And one of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to him, “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are they for so many people?”
